sections.title: Intake
missing: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund Dolos by TxPipe: Maintaining Cardano's Lightweight Data Node, Year 2, including maintenance, community support, and AI-friendly documentation/integration resources.
Recipient: TxPipe via Intersect treasury management smart contracts

Deliverables: Part-time maintainer for Dolos over 12 months after the existing contract closes, Essential codebase maintenance including dependency updates, Cardano protocol compatibility, performance improvements, bug fixing, and documentation, Community support including issue triage, review of external contributions, ecosystem-feedback enhancements, and public developer communication, AI-friendly documentation and integration resources for AI-driven development workflows
Deadline/expiry: 12-month grant period beginning after closure of the existing contract

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 540,750 ADA total, composed of 525,000 ADA for Dolos maintenance and enhancement plus a 15,750 ADA Intersect administration fee.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The on-chain treasury withdrawal reward is 540,750,000,000 lovelace to the listed 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says the core maintenance request is 420,000 ADA plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve for a part-time Dolos maintainer over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Dolos is described as a lightweight open-source Cardano data node in Rust that indexes ledger primitives and exposes multiple query interfaces including Mini-Blockfrost, UTxO-RPC, Mini-Kupo, and an Ouroboros N2C socket.
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposal states Dolos has 939 commits, 80 releases, 129 stars, 51 forks, and 27 contributors.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The maintainer scope includes dependency updates, protocol compatibility, performance improvements, bug fixes, documentation, issue triage, contribution review, ecosystem-feedback enhancements, and AI-friendly resources.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says the core maintenance request is 420,000 ADA plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve for a part-time Dolos maintainer over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states TxPipe has received 30 Project Catalyst grants across Funds 9 through 14, with 26 delivered and 4 under development on schedule, and that prior Intersect 2025 open-source maintenance funding for Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC is being delivered on schedule.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 540,750 ADA total, composed of 525,000 ADA for Dolos maintenance and enhancement plus a 15,750 ADA Intersect administration fee." though 1 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says the core maintenance request is 420,000 ADA plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve for a part-time Dolos maintainer over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
missing: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

blocking_questions: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
